title: One Day in Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **Do not try to see all of Berlin in one day.** The city is huge and spread out. Almost everything a first-timer pictures sits on one walkable spine in the historic centre, from Alexanderplatz to the Brandenburg Gate. Walk that line and skip the rest.

**Morning: start at Alexanderplatz and head west.** It is the easiest hub to reach and sits under the TV Tower. The smartest move is to join a free walking tour, which covers the whole central stretch with the story attached, then walk on past Museum Island and down Unter den Linden.

**Midday: Brandenburg Gate, Reichstag and lunch.** Stand under the gate, then walk five minutes to the Reichstag. The glass dome is a free highlight, but you must register in advance with your passport or ID, so book online before your trip.

**Afternoon: pick one deeper stop, not five.** Choose Museum Island (four of five museums open, Pergamon closed until 2027, all shut on Mondays), the East Side Gallery stretch of the Berlin Wall, or the Holocaust Memorial by the gate.

**Practical rules:** buy one AB day ticket rather than singles, check the day of the week (museums closed Mondays), book the Reichstag before you fly, walk more than you ride, and keep the evening near where the day ended. End slowly by the Spree at sunset.
